sources.label: Investegate - GHH RNS list (no announcements Jun 5-9; latest: Directorate Change Jun 3, Interim Results Jun 2)
SQC Research - record order book, new 1050p TP after ~18% post-interims drop to 920p
Investing.com - Deutsche Bank raises GHH price target on margin strength (post Jun 2 interims)
ADVFN - H1 FY26: revenue +15.5% to GBP 81.9m, record GBP 167.3m order book, A&D +51.7%

verdicts.rankRationale: No RNS or material news in the Jun 6-9 window and today's -0.64% is drift; the Jun 2 interims, ~18% post-results de-rating and broker TP raises were all known at yesterday's hold, so there is no genuinely new information to re-rank on. Small positive delta because the post-results washout left a record GBP 167.3m order book and early semiconductor recovery intact at a ~17% cheaper entry, modestly improving asymmetry for this latent-node thesis.
verdicts.bull: Record order book (GBP 167.3m, near-full FY26 revenue cover), A&D revenue +51.7%, and early semiconductor recovery validate the precision-photonics-beyond-transceivers pathway; the Jun 2 profit-taking flush reset crowding while brokers (Deutsche Bank, Zeus 950p, SQC 1050p) raised targets above the 932p price.
verdicts.neutral: Strong H1 but fully disclosed and already digested; at +51% YTD and a $0.35B cap with hold verdicts two days running, the setup is unchanged until the semiconductor and life-sciences recovery shows up in orders rather than commentary.
verdicts.bear: Gross margin fell 100bps to 29.4% on mix and germanium supply shortages - the company is itself exposed to upstream material bottlenecks - and life sciences declined; a still-rich +51% YTD multiple on a small-cap AIM name leaves room for further de-rating if H2 margins disappoint.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of the semiconductor/industrial recovery converting into order book growth and H2 margin recovery as germanium supply normalizes (next trading update / FY26 results).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Persistent germanium and supply-chain shortages compressing margins, plus post-rerating de-rating risk in an illiquid AIM small cap still up ~51% YTD.
